invariantResults.evidence: Front matter title 'Reference - Content item query' follows 'Reference - XYZ' pattern.

All API members in backticks: `IContentQueryDataContainer`, `ContentItemID`, `ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames`, `IReadOnlyCollection<string>`, `ContentItemRolesResultProcessor`, `ContentItemIsSecured`, `IncludeSecuredItems`, `GetValue<string>`. No bare code elements in prose.
Code example (lines 1094-1126) broken into logical steps with comments: building query, configuring options, executing with selector, checking access requirements.

New headings use sentence case: 'Query result container properties' (line 1024), 'Access role names from query results' (line 1090). 'ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames' (line 1028) is a code element, exempt.
Line 1090: heading '#### Access role names from query results' uses imperative mood. FIX VERIFIED — was 'Accessing' (gerund) in attempt 1.
Active voice throughout: 'Gets the code names' (line 1030), 'the property returns' (line 1074), 'The system populates this property' (line 1076), 'The processor also resolves' (line 1086).
Present simple tense used consistently: 'Gets', 'returns', 'populates', 'runs', 'resolves', 'uses', 'is only fetched'.
No British English spellings in new content.
Consistent terminology: 'member roles' throughout (not 'membership roles' or 'user roles'), 'content item' (not 'content element'), 'query result' consistent with existing page.
No unclear pronoun 'it' usage. Subjects restated explicitly throughout.
No redundant information. Lazy loading explained once in note callout. ContentItemRolesResultProcessor mentioned once in body, once in note — each adds distinct context.
No filler words ('actually', 'basically', 'really', 'just', 'in fact') found in new content.
Most sentences under 20 words. Longest (~25 words, line 1082) acceptable for a conditional-behavior explanation.
No 'please' found in new content.
Leads with property purpose, follows with behavior, then code example. Gets to the point directly.
Links to related pages: member_roles_xp, reference_content_access_settings_xp, and inpage links to WithLinkedItems and query executor config. Does not assume prior reading.
Front matter title 'Reference - Content item query' does not end with -ing form.
New section is self-contained with full property documentation (type, default, behavior) and links to dependent concepts.
Property details in structured Liquid table (lines 1032-1072) with Detail/Value columns, improving readability over prose.
No pluralized code elements. Uses noun phrases: 'member roles' not '`MemberRole`s'.
Single space between sentences. Table indentation uses consistent 4-space increments.
Oxford comma not applicable — no 3+ item inline lists in prose.
Note callout (lines 1078-1084) used for important behavioral detail about lazy loading. Note severity is appropriate.
Lines 1078-1084: Note callout has bold title '**Lazy loading of role data**' on separate line (line 1080), followed by blank line, then 3-sentence body. FIX VERIFIED — title was missing in attempt 1.
Callout body does not contain the word 'Note' as text.
Note severity matches content — lazy loading is an important behavioral point.
Valid Liquid admonition: {% note %} / {% endnote %}.
Code block uses valid Liquid tag: {% code lang=csharp title="Access ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames from query results" %}.
Property details table uses correct Liquid table/row/cell markup with header=true (lines 1032-1072).
All page_link identifiers present in target file front matter: member_roles_xp, reference_content_access_settings_xp. No hallucinated identifiers.
Anchor 'ReferenceContentitemquery-ResultContainerProperties' (line 1024) uses only ASCII letters and hyphens, starts with letter.
All link text is descriptive: 'member roles', 'Reference - ContentAccessSettings', 'WithLinkedItems', 'query executor configuration'. No vague 'click here'.
Code comments use third person singular: 'Builds a query', 'Executes the query', 'Checks access requirements', 'Populated automatically'. No final dots on single-line comments.
Comments focus on Kentico-specific behavior (secured items, role population, custom selector). Generic .NET patterns (foreach, LINQ .Any()) left uncommented.

additionalFindings.evidence: Headings follow correct hierarchy: ## (line 1024) → ### (line 1028) → #### (line 1090). No skipped levels.
Liquid table follows same pattern as existing IContentQueryExecutor config table (lines 985-1021). Code block uses lang=csharp with title, consistent with page conventions.
Not modified by this task. All required fields present: title, persona, identifier, order, license, redirect_from, toc.
All Liquid tags valid: {% table %}, {% row %}, {% cell %}, {% endtable %}, {% note %}, {% endnote %}, {% code %}, {% endcode %}, {% page_link %}, {% inpage_link %}, {% anchor %}.
Language tag present (lang=csharp), title attribute, consistent 4-space indentation.
Compared attempt 2 content against attempt 1 review. Only lines 1080 (bold title added) and 1090 (heading verb form changed) differ. No other content modified — no regression risk.
